name: Losar (Tibetan New Year)
traditions: Making khapse (fried pastries); hanging prayer flags; visiting monasteries; cham masked dances; offering torma (ritual cakes).
historicalBackground: Losar has been celebrated for over 2,000 years, predating Buddhism in Tibet. It originally marked the winter solstice but was adjusted to the lunar calendar.
description: The Tibetan New Year celebration, marked with monastery rituals, butter sculptures, and the hanging of fresh prayer flags.
